comment.body: Absolutely - he could still be wrong, and i expect we're very close to a face-ripping bounce. I will say though (and i mentioned this a few days ago), market character has changed. These moves over the last few days have not been entirely predictable based on option/hedging flows alone. Real-money selling has been coming in i.e. the long-term players that actually own underlying assets have been selling.

comment.body: It was all about NVDA earnings and coupled with Alma's intraday posts. Alma's 2/26 intraday post was bullish after NVDA earnings so I bought SPX calls right before yesterday's market close. Sold SPX calls at US RTH because SPX opened up but rejected at Alma's zero vanna 5982 and bought SPX puts, 1DTE. The market was overall bearish all day so I held through the afternoon session. Took gradual profits, held runners, and now all out. As for NVDA, price always re-test highs and lows (see chart highlighted white) so I bought puts at opening with 8DTE weekly - same took gradual profits, held runners and now all out.
